quiz.title: Pre-calculus: Unit 10 Quiz 11
quiz.subtitle: 10.11: Analytic Trig II — Equations (Calculations)
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Solving basic trig equations, algebraic methods, multiple angles, quadratic form, using identities, inverse trig, interval solutions, applications, inequalities, and comprehensive equation solving

questions.question: Solve $2\sin^2 x - \sin x - 1 = 0$ on $[0, 2\pi)$.
questions.explanation: $(2\sin x + 1)(\sin x - 1) = 0$. $\sin x = -1/2 \implies x = 7\pi/6, 11\pi/6$; $\sin x = 1 \implies x = \pi/2$.

questions.question: Solve $\sin(2x) = \sin x$ on $[0, 2\pi)$.
questions.explanation: $2\sin x \cos x - \sin x = 0 \implies \sin x(2\cos x - 1) = 0$. $\sin x = 0 \implies x = 0, \pi$; $\cos x = 1/2 \implies x = \pi/3, 5\pi/3$.

questions.question: Solve $3\tan x - 2 = 0$ for the principal value (first positive angle) in radians.
Solve $\tan x = \sqrt{3}$ on $[-\pi/2, \pi/2]$.
questions.explanation: $\tan x = \sqrt{3}$ at $x = \pi/3$, which is within the interval $[-\pi/2, \pi/2]$.

questions.question: A population of rabbits is modeled by $P(t) = 500 + 200\sin(\frac{\pi}{12}t)$, where $t$ is in months. Find the first time $t > 0$ when the population reaches 600 rabbits.
Solve $\sin x > \frac{1}{2}$ on $[0, 2\pi)$.
questions.explanation: $\sin x > 1/2$ for $x$ between $\pi/6$ and $5\pi/6$ (above the line in Q1 and Q2).

questions.question: Solve $\tan^2 x - \sec x - 1 = 0$ on $[0, 2\pi)$.
questions.explanation: $\tan^2 x = \sec^2 x - 1 \implies \sec^2 x - \sec x - 2 = 0 \implies (\sec x - 2)(\sec x + 1) = 0$. $\sec x = 2 \implies \cos x = 1/2 \implies x = \pi/3, 5\pi/3$; $\sec x = -1 \implies \cos x = -1 \implies x = \pi$.

scoreMessages.70: Good work. Review multiple-angle and identity-based solving carefully.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing — focus on factoring and using inverse trig functions.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basic unit circle values and equation-solving techniques.
